programs.name: Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Medicaid in New York provides health coverage including long-term care services for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. It covers skilled nursing facility care, home health care, and hospice services for those who qualify.[1][6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and disabled individuals meeting income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers nursing home care up to 100 days post-hospitalization with co-insurance after day 20, full home health and hospice.[1]

programs.name: New York State Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waiver Programs
programs.what_it_does: These waivers provide home and community-based services to allow seniors to remain in their homes instead of institutional care. Services are coordinated through the New York State Office for the Aging to supplement informal care.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults at risk of nursing home placement who meet functional and financial eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Personal care, homemaker services, adult day care; value depends on assessed needs, aimed at cost-effective community aging.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE offers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings, including adult day health care. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits for participants.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible, able to live safely in community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitated coverage of primary, acute, long-term care; no copays for services.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program (MSP)
programs.what_it_does: The MSP helps pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance for eligible low-income beneficiaries through programs like QMB, SLMB, and QI. In New York, it covers Part A/B costs based on income levels.[1][6]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees with limited income/ resources (e.g., QMB: ≤100% FPL, SLMB: ≤120%, QI: ≤135%).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full premiums/cost-sharing; SLMB: Part B premium (~$174.70/mo); QI: Partial Part B.[1]

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly electronic benefits (EBT card) to buy food at stores. New York offers ESAP and NYSCAP for simplified access for seniors/disabled.[2][1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ or disabled with low income; ESAP for no earned income households, NYSCAP for SSI solo recipients.
programs.benefit_value: Up to max allotment based on household size/income (e.g., $291/mo individual); higher deductions for seniors.[2]

programs.name: Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP)
programs.what_it_does: HEAP provides one-time payments to help with heating costs during winter. It assists eligible households with energy bills.[1][6][10]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; income ≤60% state median or SSI/Medicaid recipients.
programs.benefit_value: $21-$1,000+ per household based on fuel type, income, household size.

programs.name: Weatherization Referral and Packaging Program (WRAP)
programs.what_it_does: WRAP refers low-income households for weatherization services to improve energy efficiency and reduce bills. It packages applications for federal weatherization assistance.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Income-eligible seniors and families, often linked to HEAP participants.
programs.benefit_value: Free home insulation, sealing, efficient appliances; saves 10-20% on energy costs.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP provides free counseling on Medicare, Medicare Savings Programs, and supplemental coverage. In New York, it's administered through local offices for the aging.[1]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors needing plan selection help.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling, plan comparisons; helps save $100s on premiums/drugs.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via Expanded In-Home Services for the Elderly - EISEP)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote independence. Part of NYSOFA's in-home services.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, meeting income guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: 5-10 meals/week; suggested donation, subsidized for low-income.

programs.name: New York Foundation for Senior Citizens Respite Care Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for caregivers through in-home aides or adult day care. Helps prevent caregiver burnout.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of frail seniors 60+ in NYC; income-eligible.
programs.benefit_value: Subsidized respite hours/services; up to 20-40 hours/month depending on funding.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service jobs with training for older workers. Administered by NY state partners.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$15/hr NY), job training, placement; 20 hrs/week.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for benefit access, housing, consumer issues. Through NYFSC and area agencies.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations, representation; helps secure $1,000s in benefits.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living, resolving complaints. Operated by local Offices for the Aging.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home/assisted living residents and families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy, investigation; improves care quality for 1000s annually.

programs.name: Elderly Pharmaceutical Insurance Coverage Program (EPIC)
programs.what_it_does: EPIC is a state program that helps income-eligible seniors aged 65 and older supplement their out-of-pocket costs for Medicare Part D prescription drugs. It pays more than half the cost of most prescription drugs after the beneficiary pays the initial amount, and assists with premiums for lower-income members.[2][3][4][9]
programs.who_its_for: NY residents aged 65+, annual income below $75,000 (single) or $100,000 (married), enrolled or eligible for Medicare Part D.[3][4]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program unique to NY that specifically supplements Medicare Part D costs for seniors, serving over 330,000 participants.[4][9]
programs.benefit_value: Pays >50% of drug costs after deductible; helps with premiums up to $23,000 (single)/$29,000 (married) income.[2][3]

programs.name: Senior Citizen Rent Increase Exemption (SCRIE)
programs.what_it_does: SCRIE freezes rent increases for eligible senior renters in rent-stabilized apartments. It protects seniors on fixed incomes by covering increases for renewal leases, major capital improvements, fuel costs, and landlord hardships.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 65+ who are primary tenants on a valid lease, with income below local limits and rent >=1/3 of monthly income.[2]
programs.whats_unique: NY-specific program (especially NYC) that freezes rent hikes in stabilized units, not a standard federal or nationwide offering.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Full freeze on eligible rent increases, preventing financial burden.[1][2]

programs.name: Senior Citizen Homeowners' Exemption (SCHE)
programs.what_it_does: SCHE provides a property tax exemption reducing real property taxes for eligible senior homeowners. It offers 5-50% relief, making homeownership more affordable for seniors.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners age 65+ in NYC meeting income and residency requirements.[1][4]
programs.whats_unique: NYC-specific tax exemption tailored for seniors, beyond basic federal relief programs.[1][4]

programs.name: Enhanced STAR (School Tax Relief for Seniors)
programs.what_it_does: Enhanced STAR delivers property tax relief specifically for senior homeowners. It provides a larger exemption than Basic STAR for eligible seniors.[1][2][3]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded senior-specific enhancement to the STAR program, offering greater relief than the basic version available to non-seniors.[1][3]

programs.name: NYS Supportive Housing Program
programs.what_it_does: This program offers rental assistance and supportive services to homeless or at-risk individuals, including seniors. It promotes stable housing and independence.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors who are homeless or at risk, meeting state eligibility.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State program combining housing subsidies with services specifically for vulnerable seniors in NY.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Rental assistance plus on-site supportive services.[1]
